mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) directly proxy consumer demand strength. Higher retail sales drive corporate revenue and earnings growth, which underpins equity valuations. A rising RSAFS level signals robust consumption and positive forward S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If the Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS level and 20-day forward returns is below 0.05 over any rolling 3-year window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) falling below 5% signals a tightening labor market that fuels wage growth and consumer spending, both positive for corporate earnings. This threshold crossing triggers a bullish equity outlook over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in the 20 days following UNRATE crossing below 5%, the S&P 500 forward return is negative in more than half of such events, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is near zero (≤0.25%) and CPI inflation is accelerating month-over-month (>0.3%), the real interest rate deepens negative territory, encouraging risk-taking and shifting capital into equities. This joint condition boosts forward returns over a one-month horizon.
regime_hint: exceptionally accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation
failure_condition: If the 20-day return after the trigger is negative in at least 60% of instances, or the average return is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) rising above its pre-pandemic level (100) confirms the economy has entered a sustainable recovery phase. In this regime, further gains in industrial production reflect solid real output growth that supports corporate earnings and equity prices over a quarterly horizon.
regime_hint: post-COVID recovery with industrial production above 100
failure_condition: If the correlation between INDPRO level and subsequent 60-day returns is negative or below 0.10 when INDPRO > 100, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1200 thousand units indicate robust residential investment and consumer confidence. Strong housing activity leads to higher construction employment and durable goods demand, which lifts equity prices over a short weekly horizon.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of instances when HOUST exceeds 1200, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) are a broad measure of employment. Sustained growth in PAYEMS signals ongoing labor market improvement, rising aggregate incomes, and increasing consumer spending, all of which support corporate earnings and a positive outlook for equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If the level of PAYEMS (or its 3-month change) shows a rank IC below 0.05 with 20-day forward returns over any 2-year window, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: In an environment where the federal funds rate is extremely low (below 1%), any upward move in FEDFUNDS signals the start of monetary tightening. Tightening is a negative shock to liquidity and discount rates, causing equity prices to fall over a short horizon. The effect is amplified in this regime.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25% in one month
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return after a Fed funds rate increase of >25bp from below 1% is positive in more than 40% of cases, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth outpaces GDP growth over three months, it suggests supply accumulation that will eventually outstrip demand, leading to inventory gluts and margin compression. This oversupply condition predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as output is rebalanced.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in INDPRO > 3-month change in GDPC1
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return after the trigger is positive in more than 50% of occurrences, or the average return is not significantly negative, the hypothesis is falsified.
